title: -Contract: Throne Lab Portable Toilet Pilot Program

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during recess authorizing
the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments with Throne Lab, Inc. to
provide a pilot program of portable toilet services for a total amount not to exceed $70,000.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
The portable toilets from Throne Lab, Inc. have technology that provides information to
evaluate this service and decide on next steps. A delay would mean that sufficient data will
not be collected during the higher-use months of the Fall.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for the four-month portable toilet pilot program will be provided by the General
Fund (011) ($35,000) and Parks Tax Fund (138) ($35,000).
sections.current_situation:
In the summer of 2025, after reviewing a range of vendors of portable restrooms, staff
found Throne Labs, Inc., a vendor with high quality portable restrooms with electronic
access controls and monitoring capabilities already being successfully operated in Los
Angeles, Michigan, Washington D.C., and the Bay Area, including Martinez, Palo Alto,
Redwood City and Sunnyvale. Sufficient funds have been identified to conduct a four (4)
month pilot program to operate two Throne Lab portable restrooms and collect usage data
at Cesar Chavez Park and Civic Center Park in Berkeley. Staff will evaluate Throne’s
restrooms’ effectiveness, impact and comparative cost to other temporary and permanent
restroom solutions.
sections.background:
The Citywide Restroom Study of 2020 performed an evaluation of existing public
restrooms, a community input process, and research into ecologically-friendly restrooms

RECESS ITEM
Contract: Throne Lab Portable Toilet Pilot Program CONSENT CALENDAR
September 9, 2025
with the goal of providing recommended locations and types and conceptual designs for
upgrades of existing and new public restroom solutions in Berkeley.1 The study
recommends monitoring permanent and portable restrooms to provide information about
how many people use which restrooms when, and what was their experience. This
information can help the City better meet the needs of the public with optimized hours of
operation and frequency of cleaning.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Providing public restrooms may help reduce open defecation which impacts stormwater
and public health. There are no identifiable climate impacts associated with the
recommendation contained in this report.
sections.rationale:
Throne Labs, Inc. has been successful in providing effective portable public toilets with
electronic access controls and data monitoring capabilities in Los Angeles, Michigan,
Washington D.C., and the Bay Area, including Martinez, Palo Alto, Redwood City and
Sunnyvale. A four-month pilot program in Berkeley will provide the City with the information
needed to evaluate Throne’s restrooms’ effectiveness and help the City potentially expand
cost-effective public restroom amenities that best meet the City’s needs in the future.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks, Recreation & Waterfront Department, 981-6700

Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: THRONE LAB PORTABLE TOILET PILOT PROGRAM
WHEREAS, the Citywide Restroom Study of 2020 performed an evaluation of existing public
restrooms, a community input process, and research into ecologically-friendly restrooms with
the goal of providing recommended locations and types and conceptual designs for upgrades
of existing and new public restroom solutions in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, the study recommends understanding how many people use which restrooms
when, and what was their experience. This information can help the City better meet the
needs of the public with optimized hours of operation and frequency of cleaning; and
WHEREAS, in the summer of 2025, after reviewing a range of vendors of portable restrooms,
staff found Throne Labs, Inc., a vendor with high quality portable restrooms with electronic
access controls and data monitoring capabilities already being successfully operated in Los
Angeles, Michigan, and Washington D.C., and the Bay Area; and
WHEREAS, sufficient funds are available in the General Fund 011 ($35,000) and the Parks
Tax Fund 138 ($35,000) to conduct a four-month pilot program to operate two Throne Lab
portable restrooms at Cesar Chavez Park and Civic Center Park.
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the Council
ratifies the action taken by the City Manager during recess to execute a contract and any
amendments with Throne Lab, Inc. to provide a pilot program of portable toilet services for a
total amount not to exceed $70,000. A record signature copy of said contract and any
amendments to be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
